sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Velocity Financial (NYSE: VEL) CLO buys 858 ESPP shares
feed.summary: Velocity Financial's Chief Legal Officer, Kelly Roland Thomas, acquired 858 shares of common stock through the company's employee stock purchase plan (ESPP) at $18.46 per share. This transaction, exempt under Rule 16b-3(c), increases Thomas's direct holdings to 99,852 common shares. The article details the transaction, provides definitions of key terms like ESPP and Rule 16b-3(c), and includes the full SEC Form 4 filing.

feed.title: Badger Meter, Inc. Investors Have Until August 3rd to Contact Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Seeking Lead Plaintiff Role
feed.summary: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. has filed a class action lawsuit against Badger Meter, Inc. (NYSE:BMI) on behalf of investors who purchased common stock between April 18, 2024, and April 16, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Badger Meter's financial results were misleadingly driven by pulling forward customer orders, concealing weakening demand. Investors have until August 3, 2026, to apply for lead plaintiff status.

feed.title: Janus Henderson Secures $2.9 Billion Term Loan and $500 Million Revolver to Support Take-Private
feed.summary: Janus Henderson completed financing for its take-private merger by securing a new senior secured credit agreement. This includes a $2.9 billion term loan and a $500 million revolving facility. Concurrently, the company terminated an undrawn $200 million unsecured revolver and extinguished an equity warrant, streamlining its post-merger capital structure.

feed.title: Janus Henderson Announced Transfer or Voluntary Withdrawal of Listing
feed.summary: Janus Henderson Group (JHG) has announced its intention to delist from the NYSE. The company notified the NYSE of a merger closing, requested a trading halt effective July 1, 2026, and will file Form 25 to remove its shares from listing and registration. Additionally, JHG plans to file Form 15 to suspend its SEC reporting obligations.

feed.title: Beta Bionics to Announce Second Quarter 2026 Financial Results on July 29, 2026
feed.summary: Beta Bionics, Inc. (Nasdaq: BBNX) announced it will release its second quarter 2026 financial results after market close on Wednesday, July 29, 2026. The company will host a conference call and webcast on the same day at 4:30 pm Eastern Time to discuss its performance. The webcast link will be available on the company's investor relations website.

feed.title: Tri Pointe Homes, Inc. Financial Disclosures & Filings

It presents a detailed chronological list of documents spanning from Q1 FY2021 to projected Q1 FY2026, enabling informed investment decisions by offering insight into company performance and strategy.

It includes real-time stock information, key financial ratios, earnings per share data, insider and institutional ownership percentages, and various performance indicators over different periods. The data indicates a recent stock price decline, but also highlights long-term growth in some areas.

feed.title: Orion S.A. (OEC) awards director 19,150 restricted shares
feed.summary: Orion S.A. director Paik Yi Hyon was granted 19,150 restricted common shares on June 26, 2026, as an award with a price of $0.00 per share. These shares will vest the day before Orion S.A.'s 2027 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders. Following this transaction, Paik Yi Hyon directly owns 65,039 common shares of Orion S.A.

feed.title: Valmont Announces Timing of Second Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Conference Call
feed.summary: Valmont Industries, Inc. (NYSE: VMI) will release its second quarter 2026 financial results after the market closes on Wednesday, July 23, 2026. The company will also host a conference call to discuss these results and its outlook for the balance of the year. Investors can access the live webcast or a replay through the company's website.

feed.title: Vicor Corp CEO Patrizio Vinciarelli sells $3.88m in company stock
feed.summary: Patrizio Vinciarelli, CEO of Vicor Corp, sold approximately $3.88 million worth of company stock through a pre-arranged trading plan. This sale involved over 10,000 shares at prices ranging from $322 to $364, following a significant surge in the stock price over the past year. The company recently reported strong Q1 2026 earnings and raised its Q2 revenue guidance, alongside a raised price target from Needham due to an optimistic long-term financial model.

feed.title: Innospec Publishes 2025 Sustainability Report Highlighting Measurable Progress and Responsible Growth
feed.summary: Innospec Inc. has released its 2025 Sustainability Report, demonstrating significant progress in environmental performance, safety, and responsible business practices. Key achievements include reductions in greenhouse gas emissions and employee accidents, increased investment in research and technology, and substantial contributions to social value and charitable initiatives. The report underscores Innospec's commitment to sustainable business growth and continuous improvement across its operations.

feed.title: Coca-Cola ousts Pepsi as the official soda at Marriott hotels worldwide
feed.summary: Marriott International has announced a new global agreement with The Coca-Cola Company, making Coca-Cola products the official beverage at Marriott hotels worldwide. This partnership ends Marriott's 34-year relationship with PepsiCo. Marriott states that Coca-Cola is preferred globally by a significant margin and by most of its guests, aiming to enhance guest experience and provide economic benefits for its operators.

feed.title: Form 4 VeriSign Inc For: 1 July By Investing.com
feed.summary: This article from Investing.com announces a Form 4 filing for VeriSign Inc. on July 1st. It appears to be an automated news item published alongside other financial news and market data, typical of an Investing.com update.

feed.title: Chubb Unit Loses Bid to Reverse Smucker’s Peanut Butter Ruling
feed.summary: A Chubb Ltd. unit, Ace American Insurance Co., failed in its appeal to reverse a lower court's decision classifying a salmonella outbreak linked to Smucker's peanut butter as a single "occurrence" rather than multiple. The US Court of Appeals for the Sixth Circuit ruled that Ohio state law and Ace's commercial general liability policies consider the bacterial exposure one occurrence, despite the carrier's argument for hundreds of occurrences. This decision has implications for the extent of Smucker's liability coverage.

feed.title: UBS AG structured notes linked to United Airlines (AMUB) — $1,000 Notes, 15.56% coupon
feed.summary: UBS AG is offering Trigger Autocallable Contingent Yield Notes with Memory Interest linked to United Airlines (UAL) common stock, with a total issue size of $1,166,000 and a 15.56% contingent coupon rate. These notes, priced at $1,000 each, are subject to automatic call if UAL's closing level meets the call threshold, or may result in physical delivery of UAL shares if the final level falls below the downside threshold, potentially leading to significant principal loss. All payments are subject to UBS's credit risk, and the estimated initial value of $965.70 per note is lower than the issue price due to various costs.

feed.title: International Business Machines (NYSE:IBM) Shares Up 1.8% - Should You Buy?
feed.summary: International Business Machines (IBM) shares rose 1.8% to $286.16 on Wednesday, driven by its sub-1 nanometer chip breakthrough, quantum computing, and enterprise AI initiatives. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" rating with a target price of $306.94, following IBM's recent beat on quarterly earnings and revenue estimates. The company also increased its quarterly dividend to $1.69 per share.

This transaction leaves him with 426,139.0301 direct shares. These sales follow VeriSign's strong first-quarter 2026 earnings, where EPS and revenue exceeded analyst expectations.

James Bidzos sold 3,300 shares of common stock on June 30, 2026, across four open-market transactions at prices ranging from approximately $249 to $252 per share, totaling about $828,110. Following these sales, he retains a substantial direct ownership of 426,139 shares. The sales were made pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 plan.

feed.title: Zillow Fails to Escape Trial Over IBM’s ‘Single-Sign-On’ Patent
feed.summary: A federal judge denied Zillow's request to dismiss an infringement lawsuit brought by IBM regarding its "single-sign-on" patent. Zillow failed to prove that its authentication framework, which allows users to sign on through third-party platforms like Apple, Facebook, and Google, does not fall under IBM's patent for a "single-sign-on operation." This decision means the case will proceed to trial.

feed.title: Federal Judge Narrows Ex-DaVita Worker's Retaliation Suit
feed.summary: A federal judge in Michigan narrowed a former DaVita Inc. worker's lawsuit, dismissing her whistleblower claim but allowing a portion of her wrongful discharge case to proceed. The judge determined that a jury could consider whether she was terminated for refusing to participate in what she believed was illegal conduct. The ruling was issued on Wednesday, July 1, 2026.

feed.title: State Street Tapped as Default for Trump Account Investments
feed.summary: The U.S. Treasury Department announced that initial investments for President Donald Trump's newly created accounts for children will be directed into State Street Corp.'s SPDR Portfolio S&P 500 ETF. This move is part of the rollout of a new program, with four additional alternative investment options from BlackRock Inc. and Vanguard also being made available for selection later.

feed.title: Prologis scoops up vacant industrial site in San Jose
feed.summary: Prologis has acquired a 14.2-acre vacant industrial site at 455 Piercy Road in San Jose for $14 million, nearly triple its 2021 sale price. The previous owner, InSite Property Group, had proposed a light industrial development on the site, but Prologis' plans are currently undisclosed, although the company has a significant presence in the industrial real estate market in Silicon Valley and across the Bay Area.

feed.title: Transocean secures $1B contract with Equinor for three rigs
feed.summary: Transocean (NYSE:RIG) has secured a contract valued at over $1 billion with Equinor for three harsh environment semisubmersible rigs to operate on the Norwegian shelf, pending license approvals. The agreement covers seven rig years with a base day rate of $399,000, expected to exceed $400,000. This news follows a 95% surge in Transocean's shares over the past year, and analysts predict the company, despite recent losses, will achieve profitability this year.

feed.title: Vistra Corp Updates Key Credit and Financing Agreements
feed.summary: Vistra Corp has announced that amendments to its Credit Agreement and Commodity-Linked Credit Agreement are being formally incorporated into its current report by reference, clarifying how these updated arrangements affect the company’s debt profile. The disclosure highlights the importance of these credit instruments for Vistra's capital needs and risk management. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates VST as Neutral due to improved profitability and cash generation mixed with leverage risk and weak technicals.

feed.title: TopBuild (NYSE: BLD) director tendered 6,431 shares in QXO merger
feed.summary: Nancy M. Taylor, a director at TopBuild (NYSE: BLD), disposed of 6,431 shares of TopBuild common stock as part of the company's merger with QXO, Inc. She elected to receive a mix of cash and QXO stock for 6,088 shares and all QXO stock for 343 shares, rather than selling them on the open market. This transaction reflects the conversion of her shares according to the merger agreement, and she retained no TopBuild shares post-merger.

feed.title: Alcoa Announces Strategic Acquisition of South32’s Bauxite, Alumina, and Aluminum Assets for $4.1 billion
feed.summary: Alcoa Corporation is set to acquire South32 Limited’s bauxite, alumina, and aluminum operations for an upfront consideration of $4.1 billion in cash and stock, plus a contingent value right of up to $750 million. This acquisition, expected to close in the first half of 2027, will reinforce Alcoa's position as a leading pure-play upstream aluminum company, expanding its global footprint and enhancing its mine-to-metal platform with high-quality, low-cost assets. The deal is anticipated to generate approximately $900 million in synergies, be immediately accretive to Alcoa's earnings per share and free cash flow, and enhance supply security for customers.

feed.title: Millennium (CATX) reports 4.89M shares (4.3%) in Perspective Therapeutics
feed.summary: Millennium Management LLC, Millennium Group Management LLC, and Israel A. Englander have reported shared voting and dispositive power over 4,893,883 shares (4.3%) of Perspective Therapeutics, Inc. in a SCHEDULE 13G filing. The filing indicates that while they acquired beneficial ownership above 5% on June 23, 2026, they ceased to be beneficial owners above that threshold by the filing date. This disclosure details their current ownership and confirms a joint filing agreement dated June 29, 2026.

feed.title: AMARIN (AMRN) CEO Berg exercises RSUs as ADSs, 3,197 shares withheld for tax
feed.summary: AMARIN (AMRN) CEO Aaron Berg reported routine equity compensation, converting 6,250 Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) into American Depositary Shares (ADSs). To cover tax liabilities from this vesting, 3,197 ADSs were withheld by the issuer at $16.35 per ADS. Following these transactions, Berg directly holds 88,845 ADSs, with one ADS representing twenty ordinary shares after an ADS Ratio Change effective April 11, 2025.

feed.title: How Broad Index Removals At Apollo Commercial Real Estate Finance (ARI) Have Changed Its Investment Story
feed.summary: Apollo Commercial Real Estate Finance (ARI) was recently removed from several major equity indices, including S&P and Russell benchmarks, which will reduce automatic ownership by index funds and could affect its liquidity and trading. This removal comes as the company's board is reviewing strategic alternatives, suggesting a possible dissolution and liquidation subject to shareholder approval. Investors should consider how these changes, alongside a potential dissolution, will impact ARI's investment narrative, risk-reward profile, and future capital returns.

feed.title: Arlo Audio Doorbell from Arlo Technologies Inc. - compact smart add-on for existing security setups
feed.summary: The Arlo Audio Doorbell is a compact, camera-free accessory designed for existing Arlo camera systems, providing voice-first control and smarter entry alerts. It integrates with Arlo cameras to trigger recordings and send notifications, offering a lower-cost alternative to full video doorbells for users already invested in the Arlo ecosystem. This product reinforces Arlo's strategy of expanding its smart home security platform through add-on accessories, contributing to recurring revenue and deepening customer commitment.

feed.title: WUGI - AXS Esoterica NextG Economy ETF Latest SEC Filings
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of the AXS Esoterica NextG Economy ETF (WUGI), including its latest SEC filings. It details key financial metrics such as its closing price, dividend information, expense ratio, asset allocation, and performance statistics across various timeframes. The fund focuses on global equities within the technology and next-generation sectors.

feed.title: Rocket Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ: RCKT) appoints new Principal Accounting Officer
feed.summary: Rocket Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ: RCKT) has announced a change in its Principal Accounting Officer. John Militello resigned, effective July 7, 2026, and Joseph Manhede, currently Associate Vice President, Corporate Controller, will assume the role on July 8, 2026. Manhede, a CPA with extensive experience in biopharmaceutical finance, will transition into the position without new compensatory arrangements.

feed.title: Zimmer Biomet Holdings Inc. stock underperforms Wednesday when compared to competitors
feed.summary: Shares of Zimmer Biomet Holdings Inc. (ZBH) fell by 2.23% to $84.17 on Wednesday, experiencing its third consecutive day of losses. This underperformance occurred during a general market downturn, with the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also recording losses. The stock's decline suggests it fared worse than its competitors in the challenging market conditions.

feed.title: Tractor Supply Co. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Co. (TSCO) stock advanced 2.06% to $32.26 on Wednesday, outperforming the broader market which saw the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average decline. This marks the second consecutive day of gains for the company's stock. The positive performance occurred during an overall rough trading session for the stock market.

feed.title: Host Hotels & Resorts Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
feed.summary: Host Hotels & Resorts Inc. (HST) shares dropped 1.52% to $23.35, marking its third consecutive day of losses. Despite this, the stock managed to outperform its competitors on a day where the broader market, including the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average, also saw declines.

feed.title: Mobile Infrastructure Extends Loan Maturity, Tightens Interest Terms
feed.summary: Mobile Infrastructure Corp (BEEP) has extended its loan maturity date with Harvest Small Cap Partners from June 30, 2026, to September 30, 2026, allowing more time for repayment. However, the amended credit agreement also tightens terms by permitting lenders to demand monthly cash payments of accrued interest starting August 1, 2026. This related-party transaction, involving board co-chair Jeffrey Osher, offers the company flexibility on principal while giving lenders enhanced control over interest payments.

feed.title: Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG) Declines, Here’s What Wall Street Thinks About it
feed.summary: Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG) has seen an 8% decline in the past month, trading near its 52-week lows, though Wall Street analysts predict a 40% rebound. This decline has been attributed to conservative 2026 guidance, but recent sentiment is shifting due to a significant nuclear deal with Walmart. Walmart will purchase approximately 176 megawatts of nuclear power from Constellation’s Dresden Clean Energy Center for its new distribution facility, highlighting a growing corporate demand for reliable, emissions-free energy.

feed.title: Charles River Labs director James C Foster reduces stake by $16.8m
feed.summary: James C. Foster, a director at Charles River Laboratories International, Inc. (NASDAQ:CRL), sold 75,000 shares of common stock for $16.8 million at $225.00 per share, executed under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. This sale occurred while the stock was trading near its 52-week high and has delivered a 47% return over the past year, though InvestingPro analysis suggests it is currently overvalued. Foster retains significant indirect ownership through various trusts.

feed.title: Judge blocks Colorado’s first-of-its-kind price cap on Amgen’s Enbrel
feed.summary: A federal judge in Colorado has blocked the state's price cap on Amgen's arthritis drug Enbrel, citing likely "significant and irreparable harm" to the drugmaker. The judge stated that while Colorado has a legitimate interest in drug affordability, capping the price of a patented drug is not a permissible method. This ruling prevents the first-of-its-kind state-level price control from taking effect, which had aimed to reduce the annual cost of Enbrel from over $100,000 to $31,200.

feed.title: T-Mobile US (TMUS) Under Pressure As SpaceX Pushes Into Mobile Services
feed.summary: T-Mobile US (TMUS) shares have declined due to competitive pressure from SpaceX's Starlink entering the mobile services market. SpaceX plans to launch a direct retail mobile service and potentially build its own terrestrial US mobile network, moving beyond its current partnership with T-Mobile. Analysts still project significant upside for T-Mobile, driven by its 5G monetization, while also considering SpaceX's broader ambition to become a comprehensive connectivity network and possibly acquire T-Mobile.
